channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Revealed: NHSE planning for £1.5bn local deficit
item.description: NHS England has finally revealed its planned local deficit figure for 2026/27, but admitted that ongoing negotiations mean it still has to find an additional £300m.

item.title: Vulnerable children denied surgery as cancer patients prioritised
item.description: Cancer waiting time pressures have led an acute trust to withdraw an offer to treat children with complex needs in its theatres.

item.title: NHS accused of using suppliers as ‘free overdraft’
item.description: Overdue payments by trusts running to tens of millions of pounds have sparked a fresh row with suppliers, who have warned the situation is “not sustainable”.

item.title: CEOs given six months to take antisemitism training
item.description: Trust chairs and chief executives must take mandatory antisemitism and anti-racism training within six months, as part of efforts to tackle “routine ostracism” of Jewish people in the NHS.

item.title: Mackey: Cyber attack risk ‘dramatically accelerating’
item.description: Sir Jim Mackey has warned NHS leaders that cyber security is a “dramatically” bigger threat than it was just a few weeks ago, due to rapidly changing tech.

item.title: Exclusive: CQC’s ‘ridiculous’ inspections without clinical input
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has been accused of undertaking “ridiculous” inspections without clinical input which have put patients at risk, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: New commissioning structure lacks experts, president warns
item.description: There is only one senior mental health lead across the seven new regional commissioning hubs taking on specialised services from NHS England, the president of the Royal College of Psychiatrists has told a public inquiry.

item.title: Data watchdog demands answers on Palantir patient data access
item.description: The national patient data watchdog has said it will investigate how Palantir staff came to have access to identifiable patient data in the federated data platform, despite previous assurances that this would not be the case.

item.title: Midwives ‘awake for 24hrs’ at under-fire maternity unit
item.description: A maternity service has been given a “good” rating by the Care Quality Commission, despite midwives reporting being asked to work back-to-back shifts with no sleep breaks.

item.title: Consultants sought to help ICBs control £7.5bn care spend
item.description: NHS England is developing plans to procure external expertise to help it cut the cost of the £7.5bn all-age continuing care services on a “no saving, no payment” basis.

item.title: CQC gets caretaker chair after ‘regrettable’ delay
item.description: An interim chair has been appointed to the Care Quality Commission, which has bemoaned a “regrettable” delay in recruiting a permanent successor.

item.title: Trust confirms doctor as CEO
item.description: An acute trust has made its interim chief executive permanent, in the latest of a string of senior appointments in the North East.

item.title: Six trusts exit NHSE recovery regime
item.description: Six trusts have been released from an NHS England’s regime for the most challenged providers, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Ex-CEO still being paid salary by trust
item.description: A trust group whose CEO left after board relationships broke down is still paying the salary of its former boss, who is now in an NHS England regional role.

item.title: Revealed: 33,000 patients had data stolen in trust cyber attack
item.description: A hospital provider has admitted that confidential patient information relating to almost 33,000 of its patients was stolen and shared on the dark web, two years after the cyberattack took place.

item.title: First CEO role for former parliamentary candidate
item.description: A doctor who has previously run as a Labour parliamentary candidate has been appointed chief executive of Dartford and Gravesham Trust.

item.title: Substandard dialysis did not kill patients, says trust
item.description: A decision to provide substandard dialysis treatment due to “exceptional” capacity pressure was not responsible for high mortality discovered among the service’s patients, a trust has claimed.

item.title: ICB issues tougher spending rules for local trusts
item.description: Several trusts in the South West are being asked to restrict all non-essential spending due to their “challenging financial position”.

item.title: CQC slashes value of failed IT project
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has written off millions of pounds from the value of the failed IT system that was central to recent problems with its inspection regime.

item.title: Lab fault sees hundreds fast-tracked for unnecessary cancer tests
item.description: More than 1,300 patients were referred for urgent bowel cancer investigations they may not have needed after a calibration error at a shared NHS pathology service.
